meaning: n.混战场,喧哗吵闹,喧嚣,大混乱,大吵大闹
examples: Pandemonium broke out when the news was announced.
There was pandemonium in court as the judge gave his verdict.
There was pandemonium in court as the judge gave his summing-up.
Mr Reid, 47, who is manager of Warrington Town, described the scenes as "pandemonium".
On the third day, U.S. military personnel arrived to establish order amid the pandemonium.
